stream_a.title: EU lawmakers vote to extend CBAM to more than 400 downstream products
stream_a.summary: Lawmakers in the European Parliament's environment committee (ENVI) voted to extend the EU's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) to more than 400 product codes on Monday, as well as for tougher anti-circumvention measures.

stream_a.title: EU lawmakers agree to widen carbon leakage fund to downstream sectors, restrict aid to exports
stream_a.summary: Lawmakers in the European Parliament's environment committee (ENVI) voted on Monday to extend carbon leakage financial support to downstream sectors, cement, cereals, and more fertilisers, while they limited support to the exported share of production.

stream_a.title: 台版ETS該上路嗎？借鏡日韓十年教訓，揭台灣碳定價下一步挑戰
stream_a.summary: 台灣碳費今年5月底正式開徵，首年收入49.7億元，象徵碳定價制度邁入新階段。不過政府已規劃2026年下半年啟動排放交易制度（ETS）試行。環境權保障基金會與台灣氣候行動網絡研究中心近日舉辦「碳費開徵後的下一步——台日韓碳定價機制論壇」，邀集日韓專家、環境部官員及學者，借鏡國際制度經驗，探討台灣碳定價下一步。 The post 台版ETS該上路嗎？借鏡日韓十年教訓，揭台灣碳定價下一步挑戰 appeared first on ESG遠見 - 永續共好 .

stream_a.title: CFTC: Producers cut CCA net length, narrowed RGA, WCA net shorts
stream_a.summary: Emitters reduced their California Carbon Allowance (CCA) net length, while producers and managed money alike narrowed their respective net shorts for RGGI Allowances (RGAs) and Washington Carbon Allowances (WCA), according to the latest US Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) data.

stream_a.title: EU ETS maritime CO2 emissions slip in 2025 despite rise in overall sectoral output
stream_a.summary: CO2 emissions from ships covered by the EU's monitoring, reporting, and verification (MRV) system rose modestly in 2025 year-on-year, according to preliminary data, but the share falling within the scope of the Emissions Trading System (EU ETS) edged lower compared to 2024 levels.

stream_a.title: Italy’s Enel urges long-term stability in EU carbon market to drive investment
stream_a.summary: The EU’s flagship Emissions Trading System (ETS) risks scaring off long-term investment unless policymakers curb regulatory “noise” and set out a clearer plan for how subsidies and the carbon price will work together to drive decarbonisation, said Italy’s largest power company.

Climate Litigation Roundup: Fossil fuel cases move from companies to the rules that enable growth
stream_b.summary: Climate litigation this month focused heavily on the rules and approvals that shape fossil fuel growth, as claimants challenged gas projects, clean air waivers, liability shields, carbon offsetting, and corporate transition claims, and new research found a persistent accountability gap for the companies most closely tied to historical emissions.

stream_b.title: Carbon market association IETA calls for EU ETS overhaul centred on removals, global offsets, UK linkage
stream_b.summary: IETA has urged the European Commission to pursue a broad overhaul of the EU Emissions Trading System after 2030, arguing the bloc should integrate carbon removals, prepare to use international credits from 2031, reform the Market Stability Reserve (MSR), and prioritise linking the scheme with the UK carbon market.

stream_b.title: Brazil finalises delayed ITMOs regulation text -source
stream_b.summary: The Brazilian government has completed the text of a draft resolution setting out the procedures for Internationally Transferred Mitigation Outcomes (ITMOs) under Article 6 of the Paris Agreement, Carbon Pulse has learned.

stream_b.title: LATAM Roundup: CDM to PACM project approval deadline passes, Latin America in the lead
stream_b.summary: The deadline has passed for countries hosting Kyoto-era Clean Development Mechanism (CDM) projects to approve transition to the Paris Agreement Crediting Mechanism (PACM), with just one-quarter of eligible activities making the cut – and Latin America leading the charge.

stream_b.summary: Benchmark ICE CORSIA futures bounced back towards $10/t last week, climbing from two-year lows, as the European Commission's assessment of the international aviation offsetting scheme that will affect the participation of EU carriers inches closer.

stream_b.title: INTERVIEW: Future uncertain for Indigenous Peoples as Peru pledges land rights recognition
stream_b.summary: The government of Peru's recently announced pledge to improve Indigenous land recognition will only help people secure their territories in the Peruvian Amazon if this high-level statement drives official, long-lasting land demarcation, an Indigenous Kichwa leader told Carbon Pulse.

stream_b.title: Ghana must position carbon markets to address “everyday developmental problems” -lawmaker
stream_b.summary: Ghana needs to treat carbon markets as a tool for national development, and position itself as a regional hub for credible carbon credits that attract credible international investment, technology, and partnerships, parliamentarians said during a debate on the country's carbon market framework.

stream_b.title: German DAC startup opens 150t carbon removal plant in Berlin
stream_b.summary: A German direct air capture (DAC) startup has launched the country’s largest DAC plant, with capacity to remove 150 tonnes of CO2 a year, it announced last week.

stream_b.title: CDR demand to outstrip supply fivefold by 2036 -report
stream_b.summary: Demand for durable carbon removals (CDR) could outstrip annual supply more than five times by 2036, opening a roughly 50 million tonne shortfall as corporate needs struggle to be met by a market that has issued just 2 mln units to date, according to a new report.

stream_b.title: Mongolia passes first climate change law
stream_b.summary: Mongolia’s parliament has passed the country's inaugural law on climate change, creating a legal framework to curb emissions, build climate resilience, and advance the carbon market.

stream_b.title: UPDATE- Indonesia’s flagship project resumes issuance with 20 mln credits
stream_b.summary: Indonesia’s flagship project on avoided deforestation re‑entered the global carbon market with the verification of 20 million credits, one of the project managers said.
